We have our own exclusive hand-crafted bourbon that we make now. My sister, Allison, she was the bar manager. She said, we should have a bourbon, but we should make our own bourbon. And that's where I got the idea that is it possible for me to make a bourbon in Florida? After looking around, I was introduced to Cam and Aaron, two guys that own enough and come in distillery in the Funiac Springs. It's called Timber Creek Distillery. I explained them that I wanted to make my own bourbon, but I didn't want them to make it and put a sticker on it. I actually wanna get my hands dirty and get in there and understand it, especially if I'm gonna put our name on it. So they sent me home with a little bourbon blending kit that they sell at the distillery. And once I had it, we started going. What I did is I made sure I had enough rye in my mashbill that it sweetened it up and I added a little barley at the end to give it that pepper and that bite. And then I just went back and forth and to create more of a sweet with a heat balance. But it's something that a hundred proof that any whiskey drinker could appreciate and almost drink on the rocks. So I tried to hit a couple of recipes that would go with almost everything on our menu. One of the first specialty drinks I made is a Longhorn Lemonade and it has the Big Gus Black Rye Bourbon in it. And it's a little more of a summery drink, but I think it goes well with our barbecue chicken and ribs, you know? But as far as our steaks, the staples, the ribeye, New York strip, T-bone, the stuff that people come in for, we have the Big Gus Old Fashion. We've gotten rave reviews, you know? I hear a lot of, I'm from Kentucky, and that's bourbon country. But I'll tell you this, that Big Gus Black Rye Bourbon, it's good stuff, man. He did good. There's no shortage of outstanding tables here on the Emerald Coast, but when it comes to eating seafood, you want to be seated at a captain's table. Why? Because nothing. Not a view, not music, not fancy cocktails, tops getting the simplest, best seafood this area offers. That's the seafood that a captain demands. Seafood that's boat to table fresh, cooked the way the people who grew up here eat it. Captain's table in Historic St. Andrews has been serving exactly this kind of food since 1967, starting as a drive-through car hop. The captain's in the name is presently owner Mitch Holdman, who manages the important task of serving this generation of captain's table devotees. Reading through the menu is like looking through a seafood market. It seems like there's everything, from raw oysters, or baked, to grilled scallops, peel and eat shrimp, or crab claws, sandwiches like fried bread snapper or a po-boy, that if you live around here, are just your average lunch. Full meals like you wouldn't believe, complete with catches of the day, serve grilled, boiled, or fried, and sides that just drive at home that this is the best home cooking you're going to find side-by-side the best seafood. It takes a captain to know and to have access to the best seafood in this area. It takes a family business to be part of a local community, and it takes a restaurant like Captain's Table to ensure that the local seafood industry thrives for years to come, while making it delicious in the present. Captain's Table, an old Florida fish house in the heart of historic St. Andrews, Panama City. Runaway Allen's concept was built off of a tiki bar theme. And sometimes you get lost in the live music and kind of forget what you're doing and where you are. And that's a good thing when you're on vacation is not to have time to be a pressing issue. And I think live music all the way around is an added value to the dining experience. You know, I think you can go anywhere up and down the Gulf of Mexico and find a place to eat. But when you throw in a live acoustic solo act or a duo, just gives a little bit of added value to that dining experience and really helps us to bring that tiki bar feeling, you know, that we're shooting for. The traditional sing-along songs seem to be very popular at Runaway Allen. It's kind of fun to see when you're, you know, working and, you know, the restaurant's busy and the musician gets up on stage and the entire crowd just starts singing along with the musician, you know, while they're eating dinner. And one of our live entertainers, Gene Mitchell, who has a song called Runaway Allen, he sings it every night. I'd like to invite you out to Runaway Allen, come enjoy a cold beer or a cocktail, some great Gulf of the Table seafood, listen to some live music and maybe even get a chance to ring the sunset bell. I grew up just down the road in Destin where these two places were established and the Gulf Coast has changed quite a bit in that amount of time. Luckily these iconic landmarks haven't changed much at all. Pompano Joe's is a Gulf seafood restaurant with a slight Caribbean flair, favorites like coconut shrimp, garlic-crusted grouper, and the gumbo and reggae rolls are ridiculous. Our philosophy at McGuire's, if you're not proud of it, don't serve it. Our employees are people who give it down. We also have our own brewery where we make all our own beer and we also bottle our own beer. We have a seasonal beer. IPA is my favorite seasonal Christmas beer. We also have a great Christmas beer. My favorite beer is Irish Red which we also bottle. My favorite food is the New York Strip, prime beef. Best live Irish entertainment south of Boston. Sing-a-long is traditional. It's get up and clap and kiss the moose started back 25 years ago. We have moose heads all over McGuire's. We have one right above the musician's head where he sings and we call it good luck. If you come in here kiss the moose on the nose during the middle of the kiss the moose song you get good luck for the rest of the year. One of the biggest things that I think is going to just make our area explode is our martin theater which is a staple here in Panama City is being revitalized right now and all of us on Harrison Avenue are so excited about that happening and all the people that are going to want to come down here to enjoy the martin theater. Join us on the back porch with other food lovers who love Lunamuna. Lunamuna on Beck Avenue in historic St. Andrews. Hey everybody my name is Alice Masker owner-operator of Alice's Own Bayview. I was born here in Panama City, raised in Chipley a little town north of here, went to school then came back and worked with my sister Phoebe at the Harbor House restaurant which was right behind us here where the Harbor Village condos are. Did that for a little while and then went to Atlanta for the last several years and always dreamed of coming back to St. Andrews and having my own place. And here we are at Alice's Own Bayview. So went to Atlanta and worked for a couple of great companies one was Pesic Group and the other was Metro Tainment Cafes and both both were centered around the community and neighborhood type establishments where people could walk up and it's kind of like a work, play and eat type of environments all in midtown. And what I liked about those concepts were that you saw the same guest several times a week. So people felt like it was a second home and that's kind of what I'm looking for here is to have friends and family could come and hang out and watch football or eat some great seafood, walk on the marina or be in this St. Andrews environment where it's a walking neighborhood too I feel like. So for the menu how that evolved is we just knew that we didn't want to do a five page menu or a four page menu we wanted a single panel with a few great items and I think we've accomplished that. We have had a lot of positive feedback on our limited menu like our seafood platter and the group of sandwich just super hits.
